overrides.expect: \mathbf {x} _ {1} = \text { Yes,   No,   No,   Yes,   Some,   \S\S\S,   No,   Yes,   French,   0 - 10; y _ {1 } = Yes}
overrides.new: \mathbf{x}_{1}=\text{Yes, No, No, Yes, Some, \S\S\S, No, Yes, French, 0 - 10; } y_{1}=\text{Yes}

overrides.expect: \begin{array}{l} \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {t + 1} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1: t + 1}) = \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {t + 1} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1: t}, \mathbf {e} _ {t + 1}) \quad (d i v i d i n g u p t h e e v i d e n c e) \\ = \alpha \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {e} _ {t + 1} | \mathbf {X} _ {t + 1}, \mathbf {e} _ {1: t}) \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {t + 1} | \mathbf {e} _ {1: t}) \quad \text {(using Bayes' rule, given \mathbf {e} _{1:t})} \\ = \alpha \underbrace {\mathbf {P} (\mathbf {e} _ {t + 1} \mid \mathbf {X} _ {t + 1})} _ {\text { update }} \underbrace {\mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {t + 1} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1 : t})} _ {\text { prediction }} \quad \text {(by the sensor Markov assumption).} \tag {14.4} \\ \end{array}
overrides.new: \begin{array}{l} \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {t + 1} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1: t + 1}) = \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {t + 1} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1: t}, \mathbf {e} _ {t + 1}) \quad (d i v i d i n g u p t h e e v i d e n c e) \\ = \alpha \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {e} _ {t + 1} | \mathbf {X} _ {t + 1}, \mathbf {e} _ {1: t}) \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {t + 1} | \mathbf {e} _ {1: t}) \quad \text {(using Bayes' rule, given } \mathbf {e} _{1:t}\text{)} \\ = \alpha \underbrace {\mathbf {P} (\mathbf {e} _ {t + 1} \mid \mathbf {X} _ {t + 1})} _ {\text { update }} \underbrace {\mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {t + 1} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1 : t})} _ {\text { prediction }} \quad \text {(by the sensor Markov assumption).} \tag {14.4} \\ \end{array}

overrides.expect: \begin{array}{l} \mathbf {P} \left(\mathbf {X} _ {k} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1: t}\right) = \mathbf {P} \left(\mathbf {X} _ {k} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1: k}, \mathbf {e} _ {k + 1: t}\right) \\ = \alpha \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {k} | \mathbf {e} _ {1: k}) \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {e} _ {k + 1: t} | \mathbf {X} _ {k}, \mathbf {e} _ {1: k}) \quad \text {(using Bayes' rule, given \mathbf {e} _{1:k})} \\ = \alpha \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {k} | \mathbf {e} _ {1: k}) \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {e} _ {k + 1: t} | \mathbf {X} _ {k}) \quad (u s i n g c o n d i t i o n a l i n d e p e n d e n c e) \\ = \alpha \mathbf {f} _ {1: k} \times \mathbf {b} _ {k + 1: t}. \tag {14.8} \\ \end{array}
overrides.new: \begin{array}{l} \mathbf {P} \left(\mathbf {X} _ {k} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1: t}\right) = \mathbf {P} \left(\mathbf {X} _ {k} \mid \mathbf {e} _ {1: k}, \mathbf {e} _ {k + 1: t}\right) \\ = \alpha \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {k} | \mathbf {e} _ {1: k}) \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {e} _ {k + 1: t} | \mathbf {X} _ {k}, \mathbf {e} _ {1: k}) \quad \text {(using Bayes' rule, given } \mathbf {e} _{1:k}\text{)} \\ = \alpha \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {X} _ {k} | \mathbf {e} _ {1: k}) \mathbf {P} (\mathbf {e} _ {k + 1: t} | \mathbf {X} _ {k}) \quad (u s i n g c o n d i t i o n a l i n d e p e n d e n c e) \\ = \alpha \mathbf {f} _ {1: k} \times \mathbf {b} _ {k + 1: t}. \tag {14.8} \\ \end{array}
